SUPPLEMENT TO OPPOS. TO DEFT HARTFORD INSURANCE COMPANY'S MTN TO STAY OR DISMISS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS FILED BY PLAINTIFF PRICE, POSTEL & PARMA LLP, A CALIFRONIA LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT HARTFORD INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE MIDWEST'S MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS IS GRANTED. THIS ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE FEDERAL ACTION. THE TWO ACTIONS INVOLVE THE SAME SUBJECT MATTER: WHETHER DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO DEFEND. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Apr-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephen P. Ellingson, Esq., of Hayes Scott Bonino Ellingson & McLay LLP, Tel. 650.637.9100, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Philip L. Pillsbury, Jr., Esq., of Pillsbury & Levinson LLP, Tel. 415.433.8000, appearing

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HARTFORD INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE MIDWEST'S Motion To Stay Or Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens is Granted. This action is stayed pending the conclusion of the federal action. The two actions involve the same subject matter: whether defendant owed a duty to defend. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Apr-05-2012 Continued To Apr-18-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
